updated_conclusion: Reverser now has a native PE qword readback path that confirms the login-settings provider descriptors are mapped .rdata rows whose early slots point to executable .text thunks. The slot-0 materializers have zero direct CALL rel32 references, supporting the prior conclusion that 0x140679720 reaches them through descriptor/vtable dispatch rather than ordinary direct callers.

tooling_milestone.client_use_case: Read rs2client.exe descriptor/vtable rows without spinning up a Ghidra project, while annotating whether qword values target executable code or another mapped image section.

descriptor_rows_confirmed.known_role: Player-source provider descriptor consumed by the sibling settings builder path.
Console provider descriptor consumed by the sibling settings builder path.

new_observations: Each sampled descriptor starts in .rdata and the native reader annotates its first provider slots as executable .text targets.
The first two descriptor entries are duplicated for 0x140B69FC0, 0x140B859D8, 0x140B69F60, and 0x140B61B90, matching the clone/materializer slot pattern seen in Ghidra.

The 12-qword native readbacks show additional contiguous function-pointer rows after the first six entries; treat these as adjacent descriptor rows until a callsite proves they are part of the same logical table.
Direct CALL scans for 0x1401722E0, 0x1403EE390, 0x1401722A0, and 0x1400E0F90 all returned zero hits across 110696 CALL rel32 opcodes, so current evidence points to table-driven dispatch only.
next_targets: Resolve the 0x1403D8E7A stack-local descriptor pointer and compare its row shape with the confirmed provider descriptors.
Use pe-read-qwords on the type/static descriptor payloads returned by slot-3 thunks, including 0x140C51CE0, 0x140C53F10, 0x140C51E20, and 0x140C4D4A0.
Extend native PE tooling with import/export of named descriptor row summaries once at least one more unresolved stack-local provider is mapped.
